shared_properties: Vent source keeps a 16x16 px footprint that snaps cleanly to the dungeon tile grid.
Air flow uses cool cyan tones so players can distinguish vent acceleration from warm damage feedback.
differences: This sheet provides a four-frame downward-force loop with a brighter center column that signals the active push zone.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Vent size, downward force value, and abyss placement grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Cold air glow and darkness contrast grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use TextureAtlasLayout for the vent loop and attach the visual to a hazard entity that applies a downward force area in gameplay code.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/wind_vent_fx_sheet.png through AssetServer and store the atlas handle in the wind-vent visual recipe keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Glow stays inside the 16x16 bounds so tile snapping remains precise.

context.role: Animated force-field marker that communicates extra downward acceleration.
context.usage: Appears in later sections where players must anticipate sudden speed gains while descending.

deliverables.notes: Four 16x16 frames for the active vent loop.
Single active_loop clip definition for the vent visual.

acceptance_criteria: Sheet size is exactly 64x16 px with four 16x16 frames.
Cyan air flow stays centered and readable on dark stone backgrounds at 4x scale.
Loop conveys downward thrust without shifting the vent origin between frames.
